questions.q: Manmohan Singh served as Prime Minister of India during which period?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh served as Prime Minister from 2004 to 2014.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh holds the distinction of being the first member of which community to become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: He was the first Sikh to hold the office of Prime Minister.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, Manmohan Singh was the Finance Minister who led the reforms of which year?
questions.explanation: He was the Finance Minister who led India's 1991 economic reforms.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh's deck subtitle, 'The Economist Premier,' reflects his background as a trained what?
questions.explanation: He was a trained economist, reflected in the 'Economist Premier' subtitle.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh led which coalition as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He led the United Progressive Alliance (UPA) government from 2004 to 2014.

questions.q: Whom did Manmohan Singh succeed as Prime Minister in 2004?
questions.explanation: He succeeded Atal Bihari Vajpayee as Prime Minister in 2004.

questions.q: How many full terms did Manmohan Singh serve as Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: He served two consecutive terms as Prime Minister, from 2004 to 2014.

questions.q: Who succeeded Manmohan Singh as Prime Minister of India in 2014?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi became Prime Minister in 2014, succeeding Manmohan Singh.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms that Manmohan Singh led as Finance Minister are associated with which broad policy change?
questions.explanation: He led the 1991 economic liberalisation that opened up the Indian economy.

questions.q: As Prime Minister, Manmohan Singh held the office that is the head of which body of the Union?
questions.explanation: As Prime Minister he headed the Union government, the executive branch.

questions.q: During his tenure as Prime Minister, Manmohan Singh was a member of which House of Parliament?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh was a member of the Rajya Sabha throughout his term as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh led the 1991 reforms as Finance Minister in the government headed by which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He served as Finance Minister in the P.V. Narasimha Rao government that introduced the 1991 reforms.

questions.q: Before entering politics, Manmohan Singh served as the Governor of which institution?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh served as Governor of the Reserve Bank of India before his political career.

questions.q: A landmark transparency law was enacted in 2005 during Manmohan Singh's first term. Which law was it?
questions.explanation: The Right to Information Act was enacted in 2005 during his first term as Prime Minister.

questions.q: A major rural employment guarantee law, later named after Mahatma Gandhi, was enacted in 2005 during Manmohan Singh's tenure. What is it commonly known as?
questions.explanation: MGNREGA, the rural employment guarantee programme, was enacted in 2005 under his government.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh's second term as Prime Minister began after the general election to which Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: His second term began in 2009 after the election that constituted the 15th Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Which President of India administered the oath of office to Manmohan Singh as Prime Minister in 2004?
questions.explanation: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam was the President of India in 2004 when Manmohan Singh was sworn in.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh first became a member of the Rajya Sabha in 1991 representing which state?
questions.explanation: He was first elected to the Rajya Sabha from Assam in 1991.

questions.q: Before his political career, Manmohan Singh also served the Government of India in which senior economic advisory role?
options: Chief Economic Adviser and Finance Secretary
questions.explanation: He held senior posts including Chief Economic Adviser and Finance Secretary before entering politics.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh's premiership belongs to which broader phase of Indian national politics?
questions.explanation: His UPA-led tenure was part of the era of coalition governments.

questions.q: Before leading the Reserve Bank, Manmohan Singh headed which Indian planning body as its Deputy Chairman?
questions.explanation: He served as Deputy Chairman of the Planning Commission earlier in his career.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh's tenure as Finance Minister leading the 1991 reforms began in which year?
questions.explanation: He became Finance Minister in 1991, the year the reforms were introduced amid a balance-of-payments crisis.

questions.q: On which date in 2004 did Manmohan Singh first take the oath of office as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh was sworn in as Prime Minister on 22 May 2004.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh served as Governor of the Reserve Bank of India during which years?
questions.explanation: He served as Governor of the Reserve Bank of India from 1982 to 1985.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh's first general budget as Finance Minister, presenting the 1991 reforms, was tabled in which year?
questions.explanation: He presented his first Union Budget as Finance Minister in 1991, outlining the reform programme.

questions.q: A civil nuclear cooperation agreement with the United States was concluded during Manmohan Singh's tenure. It is commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The India-US civil nuclear deal, known as the 123 Agreement, was concluded during his premiership.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh earned his doctoral degree in economics from which university?
questions.explanation: He earned his doctorate in economics from the University of Oxford.

questions.q: Counting from independence, Manmohan Singh was the longest-serving Prime Minister after which two who held the office longer?
options: Lal Bahadur Shastri and Morarji Desai
P.V. Narasimha Rao and Atal Bihari Vajpayee
questions.explanation: His ten-year tenure ranks behind only Jawaharlal Nehru and Indira Gandhi in length among Indian Prime Ministers.

questions.q: Manmohan Singh's second term as Prime Minister concluded in which year?
questions.explanation: His second and final term concluded in 2014.

questions.q: As Prime Minister, Manmohan Singh chaired which planning body before it was succeeded by NITI Aayog in 2015?
questions.explanation: As Prime Minister he chaired the Planning Commission, which functioned until 2014 before being replaced by NITI Aayog.
